nodes.name: Every Hour
nodes.notes: Triggers every hour on the hour to sync GitHub issues with Notion. Adjust frequency as needed — more frequent = more API calls.

nodes.name: Get GitHub Issues
nodes.notes: Fetches all issues (open and closed) from tdill1/second-brain repo updated in the last 2 hours (1 hour buffer). Replace credential ID with your GitHub personal access token credential.

nodes.name: Transform Issue Data
nodes.notes: Transforms GitHub issue data into Notion-compatible format. Maps GitHub labels to types (Feature, Bug, Task, Documentation). Maps issue state + assignee to Notion status (Backlog, In Progress, Done).

nodes.name: Get Existing Notion Items
nodes.notes: Fetches all existing items in the Notion Projects database that came from GitHub. Used to determine whether to create new items or update existing ones.

nodes.name: Diff & Decide Create/Update
nodes.notes: Compares GitHub issues against existing Notion items. Determines which issues need to be created (new) versus updated (changed status or title). Bidirectional awareness — only syncs what has changed.

nodes.name: Create or Update?
nodes.notes: Routes items to either the Create or Update Notion node based on whether the GitHub issue already exists in Notion.

nodes.name: Create in Notion
nodes.notes: Creates a new page in Notion Projects database for GitHub issues that don't exist yet. Includes issue body as page content.

nodes.name: Update in Notion
nodes.notes: Updates existing Notion pages when their corresponding GitHub issue has changed (status, title, or labels).

nodes.name: Sync Summary
nodes.notes: Logs a summary of the sync operation — how many items were created vs updated. Useful for debugging and monitoring.
